_comment: Merge this 'hooks' block into your ~/.claude/settings.json. Fix the absolute path to wherever you put the scripts, and use 'python' or 'python3' to match your system. Hooks load at session start, so restart Claude Code after editing. Both matrix_signal.py AND manifest_resolver.py must be installed to ~/.claude/hooks/. Each hook passes a manifest MOMENT key (harnesses.claude-code in shared/manifest.json maps moment->intent, weights+pools live in the manifest — not in wait-weights.json): UserPromptSubmit->hook:UserPromptSubmit (working spinner from manifest working pool); Stop->hook:Stop (done glyph, arms idle screensaver + spawns bored-watcher); PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion)->hook:PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion (ask-question glyph); PreToolUse(ExitPlanMode)->hook:PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode (ask-question doubles for plan-approval); Notification(permission_prompt)->hook:Notification:permission_prompt (ask-attention glyph); PostToolUse(AskUserQuestion)->hook:PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion (flips busy the moment you answer); PostToolUse(ExitPlanMode)->hook:PostToolUse:ExitPlanMode (same for plan exit). NOTE: Notification(permission_prompt) can fire ~6s into a question/plan wait — if it visibly clobbers the ?/check during a plain question, drop the Notification block.
